title: T-005 — Address + Allergen Restriction modals (customer_create_modal, address_modal, allergen_restriction_modal) (11-shipping-a)
prompt:
# T-005 — Customer/Address/Allergen modals

PRD: docs/prd/11-SHIPPING-PRD.md §15.4 SHIP-003, §15.4 SHIP-004, §11 V-SHIP-SO-02, §11 V-SHIP-LBL-01/02
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/shipping/modals.jsx:27-67 (customer_create_modal), :69-94 (address_modal), :96-113 (allergen_restriction_modal)
UX: prototypes/design/11-SHIPPING-UX.md §4 (Customer Create/Edit, Address Create/Edit, Allergen Restriction Add)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Three client-side Dialog modals wired to T-002 Server Actions, with Zod validation and i18n.

## Implementation contract
1. `customer-create-modal.tsx` — uses shadcn `Dialog` + `Form` (react-hook-form + zodResolver). Fields per modals.jsx:27-67: customer_code, name, email, phone, tax_id, category (enum select), terms_days (coerce.number), credit_limit (hidden behind P2 feature flag — disabled with tooltip). Submit calls `createCustomer` Server Action (T-002). RBAC gate via `ship.so.create`.
2. `address-modal.tsx` — Fields per modals.jsx:69-94: address_type, is_default, address_line1, address_line2, city, state, postal_code (regex pattern), country_iso2 (Combobox from country reference list), dock_hours (JSON-validated textarea). Submit calls `upsertAddress` Server Action.
3. `allergen-restriction-modal.tsx` — Fields per modals.jsx:96-113: allergen_id (Select sourced from `allergen_families` ref table, 02-SETTINGS §8), restriction_type ('refuses'|'requires_decl'), notes textarea. Submit calls `saveAllergenRestrictions` Server Action. RBAC gate via `ship.allergen.override`. Inline alert citing `allergen_cascade_v1` rule (SET-050) link.
4. All labels via next-intl `shipping.customers.*`, `shipping.addresses.*`, `shipping.allergens.*` PL/EN.
5. All Dialog primitives imported from `packages/ui` only (ESLint guards @radix-ui usage outside packages/ui).
6. Error/loading/permission/empty states rendered.

## Prototype parity
- customer_create_modal at modals.jsx:27-67
- address_modal at modals.jsx:69-94
- allergen_restriction_modal at modals.jsx:96-113

Verify each modal renders the same field set, validation messages, and button row as prototype. Do not paste JSX verbatim.

## Files
- apps/web/app/(shipping)/_modals/customer-create-modal.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(shipping)/_modals/address-modal.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(shipping)/_modals/allergen-restriction-modal.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(shipping)/_modals/__tests__/customer-create-modal.test.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(shipping)/_modals/__tests__/address-modal.test.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(shipping)/_modals/__tests__/allergen-restriction-modal.test.tsx [create]
- apps/web/messages/en/shipping.json [modify]
- apps/web/messages/pl/shipping.json [modify]
- prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/shipping/modals.jsx [ref]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given customer-create-modal renders, when compared to prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/shipping/modals.jsx:27-67, then it has the same field set + validation labels + footer button row — RTL snapshot + Playwright screenshot.
2. Given the user submits customer-create-modal with missing email, when validation runs, then Zod returns 'shipping.customers.errors.email_required' and submit is disabled (not blocking via client-only; Server Action also Zod-validates).
3. Given address-modal is opened, when V-SHIP-SO-02 path is exercised (no prior shipping address), then submit succeeds and parent customer-detail Addresses tab re-renders with the new row.
4. Given allergen-restriction-modal is opened by a non-QA user, when the page-level wrapper computes permissions, then the trigger button is hidden / disabled (gate enforced server-side too at saveAllergenRestrictions).
5. UI closeout includes Playwright screenshots for all 3 modals + RTL coverage + Playwright trace per UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY; Playwright fallback documented if unavailable.

## Test strategy
- RED: 3 RTL specs asserting parity + Zod errors + Server Action invocation + permission gate.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/app/\(shipping\)/_modals/__tests__/`.
- Playwright `apps/web/e2e/shipping-customer-modals.spec.ts` captures 3 modal evidence.

## Risk red lines
- Do not import @radix-ui/react-dialog outside packages/ui.
- Do not bypass server-side Zod re-validation (client validation is advisory only).
- Do not hard-code allergen list — source from allergen_families ref table.
- Do not implement credit_limit UI logic without the P2 feature flag gate.
- Do not regress accessibility (axe).

pipeline_inputs.description: 3 modals (customer-create, address, allergen-restriction) translated to shadcn + Zod + Server Actions.
pipeline_inputs.details: Prototype refs: modals.jsx:27-67 / :69-94 / :96-113. Use shadcn Dialog from packages/ui + react-hook-form + zodResolver. Country ISO2 list from a typed reference enum. Allergen list from 02-SETTINGS allergen_families. credit_limit hidden behind P2 feature flag.
out_of_scope: Do not implement Server Actions here (T-002).
Do not implement credit_limit business logic (Phase 2).
cross_module_dependencies: 01-npd/T-001 (product FG SSOT — variance_tolerance_pct + allergen attributes; coverage hygiene per R3 audit 2026-05-14)

UI closeout includes Playwright screenshots for all 3 modals + RTL coverage + Playwright trace per UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY; Playwright fallback documented if unavailable.
